articles.title: Trump says ‘progress made’ in Lebanon
articles.summary: Trump says ‘progress made’ in Lebanon US President Donald Trump said on Thursday that he believed progress was being made between Israel and Lebanon and that Lebanon deserved to have peace. “Lebanon has been under attack for so many years and always like an underdog, and it would be really nice if it could end,” Trump told reporters at the White House. Trump told reporters in the Oval Office that he had spoken to Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and "I actually spoke to Hezbollah about it." He continued: "And I think progress is made. articles.summary: Lawmakers vote to curb Trump war on Iran ahead of similar effort on Lebanon The US House of Representatives on Wednesday voted 215-208 to curb President Donald Trump's war-making authority on Iran until he seeks congressional approval. Four Republicans voted with Democrats to pass the measure, including Kentucky Congressman Thomas Massie, who will depart the lower chamber in January after he was defeated last month in the  most expensive  Republican primary ever by a candidate with backing from pro-Israel lobbying groups.  It's worth noting that more than a dozen Republican

articles.title: Israeli military says soldier killed in southern Lebanon
articles.summary: Israeli military says soldier killed in southern Lebanon The Israeli military said on Thursday that one of its soldiers was killed in southern Lebanon, the first fatality since the announcement of a new ceasefire plan.  A military source told AFP that the soldier was killed by a missile fired by Hezbollah at an Israeli tank.

articles.title: US Strategic Petroleum Reserve Nears a 3-Year Low
articles.summary: The US buffer for crude oil is shrinking fast as the Iran war drags on. The country’s emergency petroleum stockpile, known as the Strategic Petroleum Reserve, has shrunk to levels last seen in mid-2023, after the Biden administration decided to withdraw SPR crude barrels to mitigate the oil-price impact of the Russia-Ukraine war. The global oil supply chain is becoming more vulnerable with each passing day the Strait of Hormuz remains closed. Ellen Wald, President of Transversal Consulting joins to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.summary: Israeli strike wounds two Syrians, two Bangladeshis in southern Lebanon An Israeli air strike on southern Lebanon injured four foreign nationals, two Syrians and two Bangladeshis, the National News Agency (NNA) reported. The agency said that Israel carried out a two-wave air strike on the al-Mashal area in Wata Abba, between the towns of Abba and al-Duwair, in the Nabatieh district.
